role: A focused modal tool palette that lets the operator pick a tool, configure it through a generated schema form, invoke it, and see a human-readable run status.
capabilities: Render modal overlay dialog for tools
List available tools from the tools store
Select and deselect a tool with back navigation
Seed form values from JSON Schema defaults and enums
Render an op-conditional schema form via SchemaForm
Validate required fields before enabling Run
Invoke the selected tool via POST /api/tools/invoke
Display honest run-status states: running, done, gated, error
Handle Escape key for back/close navigation
Retry tool list loading on store error
substance_why: It implements the full list-to-run flow including store integration, schema-driven form seeding, validation, live HTTP invocation, and human status feedback, with only result rendering and the mutating-tool gate explicitly deferred.
interactions_present: Click a tool row to open its detail form
Click back button to return to the tool list
Click scrim or close button to close the panel
Press Escape to go back or close
Click Try again to reload the tool list
Change form field values through SchemaForm
Click Run to invoke the tool
Disable Run while running or when required fields are missing
Show human status after run: count, empty, generic done, gated, or error
interactions_missing: Rich result content rendering (DNA tool-card archetype / renderArchetype seam not landed)
Operator-token approval flow for non-safe/mutating tools (only shows a gated message)
No raw/detailed output view for results
No search or filtering in the tool list
performance_risks: Stores the full result hits array in React state even though only the count is rendered, which can bloat memory for large responses
No abort controller for in-flight fetch requests when the panel closes or selection changes
Recomputes required-field validation and human name on every render without memoization
Repeated object spreading in onChange and value updates may cause unnecessary re-renders of SchemaForm
reuse_why: The modal flow, store wiring, validation guard, and honest status-machine pattern should be kept, but the component should be rebuilt around a real result renderer and a separate gating/approval seam.
decomposition_hints: Split the tool list into a ToolsList subcomponent
Split the detail/form/run area into a ToolRunner subcomponent
Extract the invoke logic into a useToolInvoke hook to isolate fetch, posture handling, and cancellation
Split the result region into a ToolResult component once the DNA archetype renderer is integrated
Split the gated approval step into a separate GatePrompt component
